name: Zhoug
servings: makes 1 small jarabout ⅔ cup / 150 ml
tips: The texture of zhoug is important. It needs to be coarse, as if it were made traditionally with grinding stones. It should also be very hot, so use more chiles if yours aren’t.

ingredients.item: flat-leaf parsley (leaves and stems), coarsely chopped

method: Place all of the ingredients in a small food processor. Blitz in a few pulses to get a coarse paste; make sure not to overmix. Store in a sterilized jar (SEE PRESERVED LEMONS) in the fridge for up to 3 weeks.
